schema_notes: Scenarios are designed for cloud-first digital-asset API and trading operations.

scenarios.name: API Key Replay From New Geography
scenarios.summary: A valid API key is reused from atypical geographies and user-agent context, indicating key theft or reuse.
trigger_conditions: Same api_key_id observed from 2+ ip_country values within 10 minutes.
At least 1 auth.login.failure precedes the first high-risk api_key.used event in the same account context.
Endpoint usage shifts from read-only to trade endpoints in the risk window.
expected_analyst_response: Confirm whether country and user-agent shift is expected for that account.
Isolate affected key and review all requests in the 10-minute risk window.
Escalate immediately if trade endpoints were used after suspicious authentication activity.

mitre_attack.rationale: Abuse of stolen API keys aligns with alternate authentication material usage.
Attacker actions are performed through legitimate credentials.

scenarios.name: Credential Stuffing Against API Authentication
scenarios.summary: One or more source IPs generate bursts of authentication failures across multiple accounts before a successful login.
trigger_conditions: Single IP produces 20+ auth.login.failure events in 5 minutes.
Failures target at least 5 distinct account_id values.
At least one auth.login.success follows the failure burst.
expected_analyst_response: Validate burst behavior against known scanner/noise allowlists.
Identify potentially compromised account(s) where success follows failures.
Escalate if authenticated sessions are established after brute-force indicators.

mitre_attack.rationale: High-volume failed authentication attempts across accounts match brute-force behavior.
Successful login after stuffing indicates compromise of valid credentials.

scenarios.name: Suspicious Withdrawal Request Chain
scenarios.summary: Withdrawal requests appear from abnormal context and amount profile, suggesting account takeover and fund-exfil attempt.
trigger_conditions: withdrawal.request appears with no recent normal withdrawal history for the key/account.
Withdrawal amount exceeds profile baseline (e.g., >95th percentile for account).
Request originates from new ip_country or region compared with prior 30-day baseline.
expected_analyst_response: Confirm whether withdrawal timing and amount are expected for account operations.
If suspicious, trigger immediate containment: key revocation and withdrawal hold per policy.
Escalate to IR/L2 with full transaction context and authentication timeline.

mitre_attack.rationale: Withdrawal abuse often includes account or key configuration manipulation.
Fund movement over API channels is analogous to web-service exfil patterns.
Abuse is executed through valid account context.

scenarios.name: Bot Hijack Causing Order-Book Manipulation Signals
scenarios.summary: Compromised bot emits abnormal create/cancel bursts inconsistent with baseline strategy behavior.
trigger_conditions: order.create + order.cancel rate exceeds bot baseline by 3x in 10 minutes.
Cancel-to-create ratio for a profile deviates from expected norm (e.g., market_maker spike).
Behavior coincides with user-agent or geolocation change for same bot/account.
expected_analyst_response: Compare observed order behavior with profile baseline for market_maker/momentum/arb_like.
Assess whether strategy deploy or expected volatility explains the pattern.
Escalate and isolate if manipulation-like behavior is unexplained by trading context.

mitre_attack.rationale: Manipulation of order flow/market signals aligns with data manipulation outcomes.
Order flood/cancel storm can degrade exchange or internal endpoint performance.
Behavior is executed through compromised but valid bot credentials.

scenarios.name: Cloud Service Token Misuse in Trading Context
scenarios.summary: Application token/API material used from non-standard infrastructure region or runtime identity.
trigger_conditions: api_key.used appears from region not in expected profile infrastructure set.
Unusual user_agent appears for same key/account in short period.
Token activity occurs outside expected deployment/change windows.
expected_analyst_response: Correlate token use with cloud deployment and IAM change records.
Assess blast radius across all requests by the same key in the suspicious window.
Escalate if access token use cannot be tied to approved operational change.

mitre_attack.rationale: Cloud/service token theft is directly represented by this technique.
Stolen tokens are reused as alternate auth artifacts.
Actor operates through legitimate service identities.

scenarios.name: Data Exfiltration-Like API Enumeration
scenarios.summary: Read-heavy API bursts enumerate positions/balances/orders beyond normal strategy behavior.
trigger_conditions: api_key.used read-only endpoints exceed 150 requests in 5 minutes for one key.
Read burst is combined with low-latency repetitive request pattern.
Burst originates from new source context (ip_country/user_agent) or outside expected hours.
expected_analyst_response: Validate whether read burst aligns with known reconciliation/operations jobs.
Quantify potentially exposed data scope by endpoint and account.
Escalate if volume and context indicate unauthorized collection intent.

mitre_attack.rationale: Endpoint enumeration targets sensitive account and position repositories.
Collected data leaves through API/web-service channels.
